Tips.heading: Know Before You Go • Safer Travel

tip1.title: Stay Hydrated
tip1.desc: The weather is hot and humid. Drink at least 2-3 liters of water daily, especially in summer.

tip2.title: Smog Warning
tip2.desc: Feb-Apr is smog season. Carry an N95 mask and check the AQI daily.

tip3.title: Use Ride-Hailing Apps
tip3.desc: Grab is safer than hailing random rides. It offers clear pricing and GPS tracking.

tip4.title: Currency Exchange
tip4.desc: SuperRich at the moat offers the best rates—often 2-5% better than at the airport.

tip5.title: Temple Etiquette
tip5.desc: Dress modestly. Cover your shoulders and knees. Sarongs are usually available for rent at entrances.

tip6.title: Travel Insurance
tip6.desc: Always purchase travel insurance with adequate medical coverage before your trip.

Travel.title: Travel Districts
Travel.subtitle: Select a district to open its trip data.

Hero.logoAlt: Visit Chiang Mai, Happy Mind, Happy Life
Hero.titleLine1: Let your heart wander in Chiang Mai
Hero.titleLine2: Where nature and lifestyle walk side by side

Popular.title: Popular Check-ins in Chiang Mai
Popular.description: Experience the charm of Lanna culture, immerse yourself in nature, and discover a perfect blend of traditional and modern lifestyles.

Events.description: Whether you're a spiritual seeker, an art lover, or an adventurer, we've gathered all the must-visit festivals and events for you right here to make your days in Chiang Mai more meaningful.

Map.headline:
Experience the Joy
Discover the Heart of Chiang Mai
Map.description: Escape the hustle and bustle to discover hidden charms. Stroll through ancient temples in the old city, sip craft coffee in Nimman, and breathe the fresh air atop Doi Suthep. Experience the tranquility and slow-life atmosphere that welcomes you in every season.

Seasons.title: Seasonal Travel Guide
Seasons.description: Your guide to weather and the best times to visit. Get your outfits ready, whether it's for the winter mist or the rainy season's rice terraces.

Hero.description: We've compiled these essential apps to ensure a smooth journey, covering your transportation, food, and accommodation needs.

Sidebar.title: Categories
Sidebar.tipDesc: Download offline maps of Chiang Mai in advance. Internet signals can drop when visiting mountainous areas like Doi Inthanon or Mon Jam.
Sidebar.warnDesc: Avoid downloading apps from suspicious links. For your safety, only use the official App Store or Google Play.

transport.name: Transport
transport.note: Grab offers the best coverage in Chiang Mai, while Maxim and Bolt are usually cheaper. We recommend downloading all to compare prices.

hotel.name: Accommodation
hotel.note: Agoda typically offers the best rates for hotels in Thailand. It's always a good idea to compare with Booking.com.

food.name: Food, Nav & Translate
food.note: Download Google Maps for Chiang Mai offline before your trip to save battery and data.

money.name: Finance & Payment
money.note: Most local shops, especially at walking streets or fresh markets, accept QR Code payments (PromptPay).

comm.name: Comm & eSIM
comm.note: Thai people and local businesses primarily use LINE for communication.
